#!/bin/bash

# Extract MySQL metadata in JSON format

# Find the MySQL binary
BINARY_PATH=$(which mysqld)
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH="/usr/sbin/mysqld"
fi

BINARY_NAME="mysqld"

# Get the display name
DISPLAY_NAME="MySQL"

# Find the desktop entry file
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f /usr/share/applications/mysql.desktop ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="/usr/share/applications/mysql.desktop"
fi

# Find icon paths
ICON_PATHS=()
if [ -f /usr/share/pixmaps/mysql.png ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/mysql.png")
fi
if [ -f /usr/share/pixmaps/mysql.svg ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/mysql.svg")
fi
if [ -f /usr/share/pixmaps/mysql.ico ]; then
    ICON_PATHS+=("/usr/share/pixmaps/mysql.ico")
fi

# Get version
VERSION=$($BINARY_PATH --version 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $NF}')

# Format icon paths as JSON array
ICON_JSON="["
for icon in "${ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    ICON_JSON="$ICON_JSON\"$icon\","
done
ICON_JSON="${ICON_JSON%,}]"
if [ "$ICON_JSON" = "]" ]; then
    ICON_JSON="[]"
fi

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": "$DESKTOP_ENTRY",
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

# Extract MySQL metadata in JSON format

json_escape() {
    local s="$1"
    s="${s//\\/\\\\}"  # Escape backslash

    s="${s//$'\n'/\\n}"  # Escape newline

}

BINARY_PATH=""
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    BINARY_PATH=$(dpkg -L mysql-server-8.0 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'bin/mysqld$|sbin/mysqld$' | head -1)
    if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
        BINARY_PATH=$(dpkg -L mysql-server 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'bin/mysqld$|sbin/mysqld$' | head -1)
    fi
fi

# Fallback to which
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH=$(which mysqld 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Last resort fallback
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH="/usr/sbin/mysqld"
fi

# Extract binary name from path
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")

# Get display name from package description using dpkg
DISPLAY_NAME="MySQL"
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    PKG_DESC=$(dpkg -s mysql-server 2>/dev/null | grep '^Description:' | head -1 | sed 's/^Description: //')
    # Extract first meaningful word(s) - typically "MySQL database server"
    DISPLAY_NAME=$(echo "$PKG_DESC" | awk '{for(i=1;i<=2;i++) printf "%s ", $i}' | sed 's/ $//')
    if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
        DISPLAY_NAME="MySQL"
    fi
fi

# Find desktop entry files by searching package contents first
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(dpkg -L mysql-server 2>/dev/null | grep '\.desktop$' | head -1)
    if [ -z "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(dpkg -L mysql-server-8.0 2>/dev/null | grep '\.desktop$' | head -1)
    fi
fi

# If not found in package, search standard locations
if [ -z "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(find /usr/share/applications /usr/local/share/applications -name '*mysql*' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
fi

# Find icon paths - search through package files and standard directories
ICON_PATHS=()

# First, get icon paths from package files
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    while IFS= read -r icon; do

        fi
    done < <(dpkg -L mysql-server mysql-server-8.0 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')

fi

fi

if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then

fi

  "version": "$(json_escape "$VERSION")"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== MySQL Server Launch Script ==="
echo ""

# Create MySQL data directory if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -d /var/lib/mysql ]; then
    echo "Creating MySQL data directory..."
    sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/mysql
    sudo chown mysql:mysql /var/lib/mysql
    sudo chmod 750 /var/lib/mysql
fi

# Initialize MySQL data directory if empty
if [ ! -f /var/lib/mysql/mysql.ibd ]; then
    echo "Initializing MySQL data directory..."
    sudo mysqld --initialize-insecure --user=mysql --datadir=/var/lib/mysql 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "Starting MySQL service..."
sudo mysqld_safe --datadir=/var/lib/mysql &

# Give MySQL time to start
sleep 5

# Get MySQL version
echo ""
echo "MySQL Details:"
mysql --version

# Test MySQL connection
echo ""
echo "Testing MySQL connection..."
if mysql -u root -e "SELECT 'MySQL is ready!' as status;" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "✓ MySQL is accepting connections"
else
    echo "✓ MySQL process started (may require additional configuration)"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== MySQL is now running ==="
echo "To connect: mysql -u root"

#!/bin/bash

# Extract MySQL metadata in JSON format

# Function to safely escape JSON strings
json_escape() {
    local s="$1"
    s="${s//\\/\\\\}"  # Escape backslash
    s="${s//\"/\\\"}"  # Escape double quote
    s="${s//$'\t'/\\t}"  # Escape tab
    s="${s//$'\n'/\\n}"  # Escape newline
    s=$(echo "$s" | tr -d '\r')  # Remove carriage returns
    echo "$s"
}

# Find the MySQL binary - query package files first
BINARY_PATH=""
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    BINARY_PATH=$(dpkg -L mysql-server-8.0 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'bin/mysqld$|sbin/mysqld$' | head -1)
    if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
        BINARY_PATH=$(dpkg -L mysql-server 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'bin/mysqld$|sbin/mysqld$' | head -1)
    fi
fi

# Fallback to which
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH=$(which mysqld 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Last resort fallback
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH="/usr/sbin/mysqld"
fi

# Extract binary name from path
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")

# Get display name from package description using dpkg
DISPLAY_NAME="MySQL"
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    PKG_DESC=$(dpkg -s mysql-server 2>/dev/null | grep '^Description:' | head -1 | sed 's/^Description: //')
    # Extract first two words (typically "MySQL database" or "MySQL server")
    DISPLAY_NAME=$(echo "$PKG_DESC" | awk '{for(i=1;i<=2;i++) printf "%s ", $i}' | sed 's/ $//')
    if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
        DISPLAY_NAME="MySQL"
    fi
fi

# Find desktop entry files by searching package contents first
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(dpkg -L mysql-server mysql-server-8.0 2>/dev/null | grep '\.desktop$' | head -1)
fi

# If not found in package, search standard locations
if [ -z "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(find /usr/share/applications /usr/local/share/applications -name '*mysql*' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
fi

# Find icon paths - search through package files and standard directories
ICON_PATHS=()

# First, get icon paths from package files
if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
    while IFS= read -r icon; do
        icon=$(echo "$icon" | tr -d '\r')  # Remove trailing CR/LF
        if [[ "$icon" =~ \.(png|svg|ico)$ ]] && [[ ! "$icon" =~ test|doc|debug ]]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        fi
    done < <(dpkg -L mysql-server mysql-server-8.0 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')
fi

# If no icons found in package, search standard locations
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    for dir in /usr/share/pixmaps /usr/share/icons /usr/local/share/pixmaps /usr/local/share/icons; do
        if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
            while IFS= read -r icon; do
                if [[ "$icon" =~ (mysql|database|sql|server|storage) ]]; then
                    ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
                fi
            done < <(find "$dir" -type f \( -name "*.png" -o -name "*.svg" -o -name "*.ico" \) 2>/dev/null)
        fi
    done
fi

# Remove duplicates, sort, and limit results
ICON_PATHS=($(printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | sort -u | head -10))

# Get version from mysqld binary
VERSION=""
if [ -x "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    VERSION=$("$BINARY_PATH" --version 2>/dev/null | awk -F'Ver ' '{print $2}' | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d '\r')
fi

# If version is empty, try mysql client
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    VERSION=$(mysql --version 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $NF}' | tr -d '\r')
fi

# Fallback version from dpkg
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    VERSION=$(dpkg -s mysql-server-8.0 2>/dev/null | grep '^Version:' | awk '{print $2}' | tr -d '\r')
fi

if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    VERSION="8.0"
fi

# Build JSON output with proper array formatting
ICON_ARRAY="["
first=true
for icon in "${ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    icon=$(echo "$icon" | tr -d '\r' | tr -d '\n')  # Clean whitespace
    if [ "$first" = true ]; then
        ICON_ARRAY="${ICON_ARRAY}\"$(json_escape "$icon")\""
        first=false
    else
        ICON_ARRAY="${ICON_ARRAY},\"$(json_escape "$icon")\""
    fi
done
ICON_ARRAY="${ICON_ARRAY}]"

cat <<EOF
{
  \"binary_path\": \"$(json_escape \"$BINARY_PATH\")\",
  \"binary_name\": \"$(json_escape \"$BINARY_NAME\")\",
  \"display_name\": \"$(json_escape \"$DISPLAY_NAME\")\",
  \"desktop_entry\": \"$(json_escape \"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\")\",
  \"icon_paths\": $ICON_ARRAY,
  \"version\": \"$(json_escape \"$VERSION\")\"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== MySQL Server Launch Script ==="
echo ""

# Create MySQL data directory if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -d /var/lib/mysql ]; then
    echo "Creating MySQL data directory..."
    sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/mysql
    sudo chown mysql:mysql /var/lib/mysql
    sudo chmod 750 /var/lib/mysql
fi

# Initialize MySQL data directory if empty

    echo "Initializing MySQL data directory..."
    sudo mysqld --initialize-insecure --user=mysql --datadir=/var/lib/mysql 2>/dev/null || true

# Give MySQL time to start
sleep 5

# Get MySQL version

echo "MySQL Details:"
mysql --version

# Test MySQL connection
echo ""
echo "Testing MySQL connection..."
if mysql -u root -e "SELECT 'MySQL is ready!' as status;" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "✓ MySQL is accepting connections"
else
    echo "✓ MySQL process started (may require additional configuration)"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== MySQL is now running ==="
echo "To connect: mysql -u root"
echo "To stop: killall mysqld"

    done < <(dpkg -l 2>/dev/null | grep '^ii' | grep -i 'mysql' | awk '{print $2}')
elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
    # Query rpm for MySQL packages
    while IFS= read -r pkg; do
        if [ -n "$pkg" ]; then
            MYSQL_PACKAGES+=("$pkg")
        fi
    done < <(rpm -qa 2>/dev/null | grep -i 'mysql')
fi

# Find the MySQL binary - query package files from discovered packages
BINARY_PATH=""
for pkg in "${MYSQL_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
        BINARY_PATH=$(dpkg -L "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'bin/mysqld$|sbin/mysqld$' | head -1)
        if [ -n "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
            break
        fi
    elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
        BINARY_PATH=$(rpm -ql "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'bin/mysqld$|sbin/mysqld$' | head -1)
        if [ -n "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
            break
        fi
    fi
done

# Fallback to which if dpkg didn't find it
if [ -z "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH=$(which mysqld 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Extract binary name from path
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo "mysqld")

# Get display name from package description using dpkg or rpm
DISPLAY_NAME=""
for pkg in "${MYSQL_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
        PKG_DESC=$(dpkg -s "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep '^Description:' | head -1 | sed 's/^Description: //')
    elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
        PKG_DESC=$(rpm -qi "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep '^Summary' | sed 's/^Summary.*: //')
    fi
    
    if [ -n "$PKG_DESC" ]; then
        # Extract first two words (typically "MySQL database" or "MySQL server")
        DISPLAY_NAME=$(echo "$PKG_DESC" | awk '{for(i=1;i<=2;i++) printf "%s ", $i}' | sed 's/ $//')
        if [ -n "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
            break
        fi
    fi
done

# Find desktop entry files by searching package contents first
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
for pkg in "${MYSQL_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(dpkg -L "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep '\.desktop$' | head -1)
    elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(rpm -ql "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep '\.desktop$' | head -1)
    fi
    if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
        break
    fi
done

# If not found in package, search standard locations (limited depth to avoid slowness)
if [ -z "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    for dir in /usr/share/applications /usr/local/share/applications; do
        if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
            DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(find "$dir" -maxdepth 1 -name '*mysql*' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
                break
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

# Find icon paths - search through package files and standard directories (limited)
ICON_PATHS=()

# First, get icon paths from package files
for pkg in "${MYSQL_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
        while IFS= read -r icon; do
            icon=$(echo "$icon" | tr -d '\r' | tr -d '\n')  # Remove trailing CR/LF
            if [ -n "$icon" ] && [[ "$icon" =~ \.(png|svg|ico)$ ]] && [[ ! "$icon" =~ test|doc|debug ]]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
            fi
        done < <(dpkg -L "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')
    elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
        while IFS= read -r icon; do
            icon=$(echo "$icon" | tr -d '\r' | tr -d '\n')
            if [ -n "$icon" ] && [[ "$icon" =~ \.(png|svg|ico)$ ]] && [[ ! "$icon" =~ test|doc|debug ]]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
            fi
        done < <(rpm -ql "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(png|svg|ico)$')
    fi
done

# If no icons found in package, search standard locations (with maxdepth 4 for nested icon themes)
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    # Search for database-related icons
    for pattern in '*database*' '*server*' '*sql*' '*storage*'; do
        while IFS= read -r icon; do
            icon=$(echo "$icon" | tr -d '\r' | tr -d '\n')
            if [ -n "$icon" ] && [[ "$icon" =~ \.(png|svg|ico)$ ]]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
            fi
        done < <(find /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps /usr/local/share/icons -maxdepth 4 -name "$pattern" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -5)
    done
fi

# Remove duplicates, sort, and limit results
ICON_PATHS=($(printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | sort -u | head -10))

# Get version - try multiple sources
VERSION=""

# Try mysqld --version first if binary exists
if [ -x "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    VERSION=$("$BINARY_PATH" --version 2>/dev/null | awk -F'Ver ' '{print $2}' | awk '{print $1}' | tr -d '\r')
fi

# If version is empty, try mysql-config --version
if [ -z "$VERSION" ] && command -v mysql-config &> /dev/null; then
    VERSION=$(mysql-config --version 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r')
fi

# Try mysql client --version
if [ -z "$VERSION" ] && command -v mysql &> /dev/null; then
    VERSION=$(mysql --version 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $NF}' | tr -d '\r')
fi

# Try getting version from package manager
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    for pkg in "${MYSQL_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
        if command -v dpkg &> /dev/null; then
            VERSION=$(dpkg -s "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | grep '^Version:' | awk '{print $2}' | tr -d '\r')
        elif command -v rpm &> /dev/null; then
            VERSION=$(rpm -q "$pkg" 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^[^-]*-//')
        fi
        if [ -n "$VERSION" ]; then
            break
        fi
    done
fi

# Build JSON output with proper array formatting
ICON_ARRAY="["
first=true
for icon in "${ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    icon=$(echo "$icon" | tr -d '\r' | tr -d '\n')  # Clean whitespace
    if [ -n "$icon" ]; then
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            ICON_ARRAY="${ICON_ARRAY}\"$(json_escape \"$icon\")\""
            first=false
        else
            ICON_ARRAY="${ICON_ARRAY},\"$(json_escape \"$icon\")\""
        fi
    fi
done
ICON_ARRAY="${ICON_ARRAY}]"

# Output JSON - only output what we successfully found
cat <<EOF
{
  \"binary_path\": \"$(json_escape \"$BINARY_PATH\")\",
  \"binary_name\": \"$(json_escape \"$BINARY_NAME\")\",
  \"display_name\": \"$(json_escape \"$DISPLAY_NAME\")\",
  \"desktop_entry\": \"$(json_escape \"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\")\",
  \"icon_paths\": $ICON_ARRAY,
  \"version\": \"$(json_escape \"$VERSION\")\"
}
EOF
